question_text: A astronaut on the Moon has a mass of 80 kg. The gravitational field strength on the Moon is 1.6 N/kg. Calculate the gravitational force (weight) acting on the astronaut on the Moon.
explanation: Weight is calculated using the formula: W = m × g, where m is mass in kg and g is gravitational field strength in N/kg. So 80 kg × 1.6 N/kg = 128 N. The Moon's gravity is much weaker than Earth's (10 N/kg), so the astronaut weighs much less there.
